story.title: Europe's Hottest June: 1,300 Deaths, 7 All-Time Records, and a Continent Unprepared for the Heat

countries.name: Hungary
key_facts: Remote work ordered for public sector

countries.name: Slovakia
key_facts: 41.0°C all-time record in Turna nad Bodvou

countries.name: United Kingdom
key_facts: Red warnings 3 consecutive days — first time
Gas plants cut ~2.5 GW due to heat

countries.name: Belgium
key_facts: Older trains without AC removed from service

countries.name: Luxembourg
key_facts: Track buckling between Berchem and Howald

countries.name: Ireland
key_facts: Among 29+ countries affected by June heatwave

organizations.name: World Health Organization
organizations.role: Reported 1,300+ excess deaths, warned Europe is fastest-warming continent

organizations.name: World Weather Attribution
organizations.role: Climate attribution study found heatwave 'virtually impossible' without climate change

organizations.name: World Meteorological Organization
organizations.role: UN weather agency tracking seasonal climate outlook

organizations.name: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
organizations.role: EU public health agency monitoring heat-related mortality

organizations.name: Électricité de France (EDF)
organizations.role: Nuclear plant operator — Golfech shutdown, €600M/year adaptation

organizations.name: SNCF
organizations.role: French rail operator — withdrew 10% of Paris trains

organizations.name: Deutsche Bahn
organizations.role: German rail operator — halted nonessential travel, GSM-R failure

organizations.name: Copernicus Climate Change Service
organizations.role: EU satellite climate monitoring — ERA5 temperature data, early warning systems

organizations.name: Allianz Trade
organizations.role: Economic impact projections — $240B France losses by 2030

organizations.name: Climate Analytics
organizations.role: Research: household income reduction ~3% from heat/drought

organizations.name: Imperial College London
organizations.role: WWA attribution study host institution

organizations.name: EU Joint Research Centre
organizations.role: MARS crop monitoring — maize and potato yield projections

organizations.name: Météo-France
organizations.role: French national weather service — recorded 44.3°C and national thermal indicator

organizations.name: UK Met Office
organizations.role: British national weather service — issued first 3 consecutive red warnings

organizations.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD)
organizations.role: German Weather Service — 252 stations broke records on June 28

organizations.name: Santé Publique France
organizations.role: Public Health France — reported ~1,000 excess deaths, 1,400+/day at peak

organizations.name: SNCB/Infrabel
organizations.role: Belgian rail operators — cut 100 trains/day, removed older rolling stock

organizations.name: CFL
organizations.role: Luxembourg national railway — reported track buckling between Berchem and Howald

organizations.name: European Commission
organizations.role: Announced heat action plan review for July 2026

organizations.name: European Central Bank
organizations.role: Co-authored study on EU output loss 0.3% in 2025 due to heatwaves

organizations.name: University of Mannheim
organizations.role: Co-authored ECB study on heatwave economic output losses

organizations.name: International Energy Agency
organizations.role: AC penetration statistics — 20% Europe vs 90% US

organizations.name: Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
organizations.role: International body assessing climate science

organizations.name: International Atomic Energy Agency
organizations.role: Nuclear safety and regulation

organizations.name: Climameter
organizations.role: Attributable temperature anomaly analysis consortium

organizations.name: MIT Technology Review
organizations.role: Published analysis of AC penetration and adaptation gaps

events.name: May 2026 European Heatwave
events.significance: May records across UK, Ireland, Portugal — precursor to June event

events.name: 2003 European Heatwave
events.significance: First major climate wake-up call for Europe, concentrated in 4-5 countries

events.name: 2022 European Heatwave
events.significance: Hottest summer on record at the time, affected 15+ countries

events.name: July 2026 US Heatwave
events.significance: Simultaneous heat dome over United States intensifying alongside European event

policies.name: EU Heat Health Action Plans
policies.gap: Only some countries have comprehensive plans; absent or underdeveloped in Eastern Europe

policies.name: Paris Agreement
policies.relevant: 1.4°C warming limit being breached as Europe heats at twice global average

policies.name: EU Early Warning Systems
policies.description: Copernicus Climate Change Service provides seasonal outlooks and real-time monitoring

policies.name: EU Infrastructure Resilience Standards
policies.description: Expected 2027 — heat-proof standards for rail, energy, and buildings

concepts.name: Heat dome
concepts.description: Stationary high-pressure system that traps hot air over a region

concepts.name: Omega block
concepts.description: Persistent weather pattern (shaped like Greek letter Ω) that locks heatwaves in place

concepts.name: Climate attribution science
concepts.description: Methodology to quantify climate change's role in extreme weather events

concepts.name: Wet Bulb Globe Temperature
concepts.description: Heat stress measure combining temperature, humidity, wind, and solar radiation

concepts.name: Universal Thermal Climate Index (UTCI)
concepts.description: Heat stress metric accounting for temperature, humidity, wind, and solar radiation; 45% of 854 European cities broke UTCI records

concepts.name: National thermal indicator
concepts.description: Average temperature across a network of representative stations; France reached 30.0°C

concepts.name: Residual load
concepts.description: Demand met by dispatchable generation after renewables; Germany reached 51.5 GW

concepts.name: Heatwave energy paradox
concepts.description: Grid needs more power when thermal generation can produce less due to cooling constraints
